spec_match.rationale: The page now feature-detects SharedWorker, runs a real same-URL/name SharedWorker probe when exposed, and makes the persistence simulation distinguish companion-client reloads from last-client termination.

interactive.rationale: The counter, reload simulator, real constructor probe, reload-condition selector, reconnect-delay range, companion-client toggle, reset, and fallback controls all change visible state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (android, available, case, chrome, client, connected, counter, least, long, reload, reloads, sharedworker, worker).

edge_cases.rationale: The page exposes unsupported-browser fallback, last-client-close reset, delayed reconnect, companion-client survival, and probe timeout/constructor error handling.

references.rationale: The references section includes chromestatus.com/feature/6265472244514816, the HTML SharedWorker spec, and the WHATWG Android discussion.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 60 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

summary: The SharedWorker-on-Android persistence page now clears the major reference gap and the related interaction/spec concerns by adding concept-level references, a real SharedWorker probe, reload-condition controls, and explicit fallback/last-client edge states.
